fr.summary: Une nouvelle étude intègre les modèles de prévision avec l'optimisation des stocks en utilisant les données de Walmart, montrant que les modèles d'apprentissage profond comme CNN temporel et LSTM réduisent considérablement les coûts de la chaîne d'approvisionnement par rapport aux approches statistiques traditionnelles.

de.title: Jenseits der Genauigkeit: Bewertung von Prognosmodellen durch mehrstufige Bestandskosten
de.summary: Eine neue Studie integriert Prognosmodelle mit Bestandsoptimierung unter Verwendung von Walmart-Daten und zeigt, dass Deep-Learning-Modelle wie Temporal CNN und LSTM die Lieferkettenkosten erheblich reduzieren, verglichen mit traditionellen statistischen Ansätzen.

es.title: Más allá de la precisión: Evaluación de modelos de pronóstico por costos de inventario multinivel
es.summary: Un nuevo estudio integra modelos de pronóstico con optimización de inventario utilizando datos de Walmart, mostrando que modelos de aprendizaje profundo como CNN temporal y LSTM reducen significativamente los costos de la cadena de suministro en comparación con enfoques estadísticos tradicionales.

title: Is Conformal Factuality for RAG-based LLMs Robust? Novel Metrics and Systematic Insights
summary: While conformal factuality can reduce hallucinations in RAG-based LLMs, research shows the approach suffers from limited usefulness and fragility under distribution shifts, highlighting the need for more robust reliability methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.16817v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) frequently hallucinate, limiting their reliability in knowledge-intensive applications. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and conformal factuality have emerged as potential ways to address this limitation. While RAG aims to ground responses in retrieved evidence, it provides no statistical guarantee that the final output is correct. Conformal factuality filtering offers distribution-free statistical reliability by scoring and filtering atomic claims using a threshold calibrated on held-out data, however, the informativeness of the final output is not guaranteed. We systematically analyze the reliability and usefulness of conformal factuality for RAG-based LLMs across generation, scoring, calibration, robustness, and efficiency. We propose novel informativeness-aware metrics that better reflect task utility under conformal filtering. Across three benchmarks and multiple model families, we find that (i) conformal filtering suffers from low usefulness at high factuality levels due to vacuous outputs, (ii) conformal factuality guarantee is not robust to distribution shifts and distractors, highlighting the limitation that requires calibration data to closely match deployment conditions, and (iii) lightweight entailment-based verifiers match or outperform LLM-based model confidence scorers while requiring over $100\times$ fewer FLOPs. Overall, our results expose factuality-informativeness trade-offs and fragility of conformal filtering framework under distribution shifts and distractors, highlighting the need for new approaches for reliability with robustness and usefulness as key metrics, and provide actionable guidance for building RAG pipelines that are both reliable and computationally efficient.

zh.title: 检索增强生成大语言模型的符合性真实性是否稳健？新指标和系统洞察
zh.summary: 虽然符合性真实性可以减少基于RAG的大语言模型的幻觉，但研究表明该方法在分布转移下存在可用性有限和脆性问题，突显了需要更强大可靠性方法的必要性。

fr.title: La factualité conforme pour les LLM basés sur RAG est-elle robuste ? Nouvelles métriques et perspectives systématiques
fr.summary: Bien que la factualité conforme puisse réduire les hallucinations des LLM basés sur RAG, la recherche montre que l'approche souffre d'une utilité limitée et d'une fragilité sous les décalages de distribution, mettant en évidence le besoin de méthodes de fiabilité plus robustes.

de.title: Ist konforme Faktizität für RAG-basierte LLMs robust? Neuartige Metriken und systematische Erkenntnisse
de.summary: Während konforme Faktizität Halluzinationen in RAG-basierten LLMs reduzieren kann, zeigt die Forschung, dass der Ansatz unter Verteilungsverschiebungen an begrenzter Brauchbarkeit und Zerbrechlichkeit leidet, was auf die Notwendigkeit robusterer Zuverlässigkeitsmethoden hinweist.

es.title: ¿Es la facticidad conforme para LLM basados en RAG robusta? Nuevas métricas e perspectivas sistemáticas
es.summary: Si bien la facticidad conforme puede reducir alucinaciones en LLM basados en RAG, la investigación muestra que el enfoque sufre de utilidad limitada y fragilidad bajo cambios de distribución, destacando la necesidad de métodos de confiabilidad más robustos.

title: Surg$\Sigma$: A Spectrum of Large-Scale Multimodal Data and Foundation Models for Surgical Intelligence
summary: SurgΣ introduces a large-scale multimodal dataset with over 5.98 million conversations covering diverse surgical tasks, enabling foundation models to better generalize across institutions and improve surgical care safety.
content:
arXiv:2603.16822v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Surgical intelligence has the potential to improve the safety and consistency of surgical care, yet most existing surgical AI frameworks remain task-specific and struggle to generalize across procedures and institutions. Although multimodal foundation models, particularly multimodal large language models, have demonstrated strong cross-task capabilities across various medical domains, their advancement in surgery remains constrained by the lack of large-scale, systematically curated multimodal data. To address this challenge, we introduce Surg$\Sigma$, a spectrum of large-scale multimodal data and foundation models for surgical intelligence. At the core of this framework lies Surg$\Sigma$-DB, a large-scale multimodal data foundation designed to support diverse surgical tasks. Surg$\Sigma$-DB consolidates heterogeneous surgical data sources (including open-source datasets, curated in-house clinical collections and web-source data) into a unified schema, aiming to improve label consistency and data standardization across heterogeneous datasets. Surg$\Sigma$-DB spans 6 clinical specialties and diverse surgical types, providing rich image- and video-level annotations across 18 practical surgical tasks covering understanding, reasoning, planning, and generation, at an unprecedented scale (over 5.98M conversations). Beyond conventional multimodal conversations, Surg$\Sigma$-DB incorporates hierarchical reasoning annotations, providing richer semantic cues to support deeper contextual understanding in complex surgical scenarios. We further provide empirical evidence through recently developed surgical foundation models built upon Surg$\Sigma$-DB, illustrating the practical benefits of large-scale multimodal annotations, unified semantic design, and structured reasoning annotations for improving cross-task generalization and interpretability.

zh.title: 外科Σ：大规模多模态数据和外科智能基础模型的光谱
zh.summary: SurgΣ推出了一个包含超过598万个对话的大规模多模态数据集，涵盖多种外科任务，使基础模型能够更好地跨机构推广，并改进手术护理安全。

fr.title: SurgΣ : Un spectre de données multimodales à grande échelle et de modèles fondamentaux pour l'intelligence chirurgicale
fr.summary: SurgΣ introduit un ensemble de données multimodal à grande échelle avec plus de 5,98 millions de conversations couvrant diverses tâches chirurgicales, permettant aux modèles fondamentaux de mieux généraliser entre les institutions et d'améliorer la sécurité des soins chirurgicaux.

de.title: SurgΣ: Ein Spektrum großflächiger multimodaler Daten und Grundmodelle für chirurgische Intelligenz
de.summary: SurgΣ führt einen großflächigen multimodalen Datensatz mit über 5,98 Millionen Gesprächen ein, der vielfältige chirurgische Aufgaben abdeckt und es Grundmodellen ermöglicht, besser über Institutionen hinweg zu verallgemeinern und die Sicherheit der chirurgischen Versorgung zu verbessern.

es.title: SurgΣ: Un espectro de datos multimodales a gran escala y modelos fundamentales para inteligencia quirúrgica
es.summary: SurgΣ introduce un conjunto de datos multimodal a gran escala con más de 5,98 millones de conversaciones que cubren diversas tareas quirúrgicas, permitiendo que los modelos fundamentales se generalicen mejor entre instituciones y mejoren la seguridad de la atención quirúrgica.

title: Prompt Programming for Cultural Bias and Alignment of Large Language Models
summary: New research demonstrates that culture-specific prompt optimization using DSPy can systematically reduce cultural misalignment in open-weight LLMs, suggesting programmatic tuning is more stable than manual engineering.
content:
arXiv:2603.16827v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Culture shapes reasoning, values, prioritization, and strategic decision-making, yet large language models (LLMs) often exhibit cultural biases that misalign with target populations. As LLMs are increasingly used for strategic decision-making, policy support, and document engineering tasks such as summarization, categorization, and compliance-oriented auditing, improving cultural alignment is important for ensuring that downstream analyses and recommendations reflect target-population value profiles rather than default model priors. Previous work introduced a survey-grounded cultural alignment framework and showed that culture-specific prompting can reduce misalignment, but it primarily evaluated proprietary models and relied on manual prompt engineering. In this paper, we validate and extend that framework by reproducing its social sciences survey based projection and distance metrics on open-weight LLMs, testing whether the same cultural skew and benefits of culture conditioning persist outside closed LLM systems. Building on this foundation, we introduce use of prompt programming with DSPy for this problem-treating prompts as modular, optimizable programs-to systematically tune cultural conditioning by optimizing against cultural-distance objectives. In our experiments, we show that prompt optimization often improves upon cultural prompt engineering, suggesting prompt compilation with DSPy can provide a more stable and transferable route to culturally aligned LLM responses.

zh.title: 提示词编程用于大语言模型的文化偏见和对齐
zh.summary: 新研究表明，使用DSPy进行文化特定的提示词优化可以系统地减少开源权重LLMs的文化不对齐，表明程序化调优比手动工程更稳定。

fr.title: Programmation par invite pour le biais culturel et l'alignement des grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: De nouvelles recherches démontrent que l'optimisation des invites spécifiques à la culture utilisant DSPy peut réduire systématiquement le désalignement culturel dans les LLMs à poids ouvert, suggérant que le réglage programmatique est plus stable que l'ingénierie manuelle.

de.title: Prompt-Programmierung für kulturelle Voreingenommenheit und Ausrichtung großer Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Neue Forschungen zeigen, dass die kulturspezifische Prompt-Optimierung mit DSPy systematisch kulturelle Fehlausrichtungen in Open-Weight-LLMs reduzieren kann und programmgesteuerte Optimierung stabiler als manuelle Konstruktion ist.

es.title: Programación de Prompts para Sesgo Cultural y Alineación de Modelos de Lenguaje Grande
es.summary: Nuevas investigaciones demuestran que la optimización de prompts específica de la cultura usando DSPy puede reducir sistemáticamente la desalineación cultural en LLMs de peso abierto, sugiriendo que el ajuste programático es más estable que la ingeniería manual.

title: Learning to Present: Inverse Specification Rewards for Agentic Slide Generation
summary: SlideRL introduces a reinforcement learning approach where LLM agents learn to generate professional presentations using an inverse specification reward, achieving 91.2% of Opus 4.6 quality with a 7B parameter model.
content:
arXiv:2603.16839v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Automated presentation generation remains a challenging task requiring coherent content creation, visual design, and audience-aware communication. This work proposes an OpenEnv-compatible reinforcement learning environment where LLM agents learn to research topics, plan content, and generate professional HTML slide presentations through tool use. We introduce a multi-component reward system combining structural validation, render quality assessment, LLM-based aesthetic scoring, content quality metrics, and an inverse specification reward that measures how faithfully generated slides convey their intended purpose. The inverse specification reward, an "inverse task" where an LLM attempts to recover the original specification from generated slides, provides a holistic quality signal. Our approach fine-tunes Qwen2.5-Coder-7B via GRPO, training only 0.5% of parameters on prompts derived from expert demonstrations collected using Claude Opus 4.6. Experiments on 48 diverse business briefs across six models demonstrate that our fine-tuned 7B model achieves 91.2% of Claude Opus 4.6's quality while improving 33.1% over the base model. The six-model comparison reveals that instruction adherence and tool-use compliance, rather than raw parameter count, determine agentic task performance. We contribute SlideRL, an open-source dataset of 288 multi-turn rollout trajectories across all six models: https://huggingface.co/datasets/KarthikRagunathAnandaKumar/sliderl-multi-turn-rollouts Code: https://github.com/pushing-the-frontier/slide-forge-llm

zh.title: 学习呈现：代理幻灯片生成的反向规范奖励
zh.summary: SlideRL引入了一种强化学习方法，其中LLM代理学习使用反向规范奖励生成专业演示文稿，用7B参数模型达到91.2%的Opus 4.6质量。

fr.title: Apprendre à Présenter : Récompenses de Spécification Inverse pour la Génération de Diapositives Agentives
fr.summary: SlideRL introduit une approche d'apprentissage par renforcement où les agents LLM apprennent à générer des présentations professionnelles en utilisant une récompense de spécification inverse, atteignant 91,2 % de la qualité d'Opus 4.6 avec un modèle à 7 milliards de paramètres.

de.title: Lernen zu präsentieren: Inverse Spezifikationsbelohnungen für agentengesteuerte Foliengenerierung
de.summary: SlideRL führt einen Reinforcement-Learning-Ansatz ein, bei dem LLM-Agenten lernen, professionelle Präsentationen mit einer inversen Spezifikationsbelohnung zu generieren und dabei 91,2 % der Qualität von Opus 4.6 mit einem 7-Milliarden-Parameter-Modell erreichen.

es.title: Aprender a Presentar: Recompensas de Especificación Inversa para la Generación de Diapositivas Agenticas
es.summary: SlideRL introduce un enfoque de aprendizaje por refuerzo donde los agentes LLM aprenden a generar presentaciones profesionales utilizando una recompensa de especificación inversa, logrando el 91,2% de la calidad de Opus 4.6 con un modelo de 7 mil millones de parámetros.

title: Internalizing Agency from Reflective Experience
summary: LEAFE enables LLM agents to learn recovery strategies from reflective experience by backtracking and exploring alternatives, improving long-horizon task performance with up to 14% gains in Pass@k metrics.
content:
arXiv:2603.16843v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents that must plan, act, and recover from mistakes through long-horizon interaction with environments that provide rich feedback. However, prevailing outcome-driven post-training methods (e.g., RL with verifiable rewards) primarily optimize final success signals, leaving rich environment feedback underutilized. Consequently, they often lead to distribution sharpening: the policy becomes better at reproducing a narrow set of already-successful behaviors, while failing to improve the feedback-grounded agency needed to expand problem-solving capacity (e.g., Pass@k) in long-horizon settings.
  To address this, we propose LEAFE (Learning Feedback-Grounded Agency from Reflective Experience), a framework that internalizes recovery agency from reflective experience. Specifically, during exploration, the agent summarizes environment feedback into actionable experience, backtracks to earlier decision points, and explores alternative branches with revised actions. We then distill these experience-guided corrections into the model through supervised fine-tuning, enabling the policy to recover more effectively in future interactions. Across a diverse set of interactive coding and agentic tasks under fixed interaction budgets, LEAFE consistently improves Pass@1 over the base model and achieves higher Pass@k than outcome-driven baselines (GRPO) and experience-based methods such as Early Experience, with gains of up to 14% on Pass@128.

zh.title: 从反思性体验中内化代理能力
zh.summary: LEAFE使LLM代理能够通过回溯和探索替代方案从反思性体验中学习恢复策略，改进长期任务性能，Pass@k指标的收益高达14%。

fr.title: Internaliser l'agentivité à partir de l'expérience réflexive
fr.summary: LEAFE permet aux agents LLM d'apprendre des stratégies de récupération à partir d'une expérience réflexive en faisant marche arrière et en explorant des alternatives, améliorant les performances des tâches à long horizon avec jusqu'à 14 % de gains dans les métriques Pass@k.

de.title: Internalisierung von Agentur aus reflektiver Erfahrung
de.summary: LEAFE ermöglicht es LLM-Agenten, Wiederherstellungsstrategien aus reflektiver Erfahrung durch Backtracking und Erkundung von Alternativen zu erlernen und verbessert die Leistung von Long-Horizon-Aufgaben mit Gewinnen von bis zu 14 % bei Pass@k-Metriken.

es.title: Internalizando la Agencia desde la Experiencia Reflexiva
es.summary: LEAFE permite que los agentes LLM aprendan estrategias de recuperación de la experiencia reflexiva mediante retroceso y exploración de alternativas, mejorando el rendimiento de tareas de largo horizonte con ganancias de hasta 14% en métricas Pass@k.

title: SocialOmni: Benchmarking Audio-Visual Social Interactivity in Omni Models
summary: SocialOmni benchmarks how well multimodal LLMs handle conversational interaction across speaker identification, interruption timing, and generation, revealing significant variance in social-interaction capabilities across leading models.
content:
arXiv:2603.16859v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Omni-modal large language models (OLMs) redefine human-machine interaction by natively integrating audio, vision, and text. However, existing OLM benchmarks remain anchored to static, accuracy-centric tasks, leaving a critical gap in assessing social interactivity, the fundamental capacity to navigate dynamic cues in natural dialogues. To this end, we propose SocialOmni, a comprehensive benchmark that operationalizes the evaluation of this conversational interactivity across three core dimensions: (i) speaker separation and identification (who is speaking), (ii) interruption timing control (when to interject), and (iii) natural interruption generation (how to phrase the interruption). SocialOmni features 2,000 perception samples and a quality-controlled diagnostic set of 209 interaction-generation instances with strict temporal and contextual constraints, complemented by controlled audio-visual inconsistency scenarios to test model robustness. We benchmarked 12 leading OLMs, which uncovers significant variance in their social-interaction capabilities across models. Furthermore, our analysis reveals a pronounced decoupling between a model's perceptual accuracy and its ability to generate contextually appropriate interruptions, indicating that understanding-centric metrics alone are insufficient to characterize conversational social competence. More encouragingly, these diagnostics from SocialOmni yield actionable signals for bridging the perception-interaction divide in future OLMs.

zh.title: SocialOmni：多模态模型中的音视频社交互动基准测试
zh.summary: SocialOmni基准测试多模态大语言模型如何处理跨越说话人识别、中断时机和生成的对话交互，揭示了领先模型之间社交互动能力的显著差异。

fr.title: SocialOmni : Évaluation comparative de l'interactivité sociale audiovisuelle dans les modèles omnimodes
fr.summary: SocialOmni évalue la capacité des modèles de langage multimodaux à gérer l'interaction conversationnelle couvrant l'identification du locuteur, le timing des interruptions et la génération, révélant une variance significative dans les capacités d'interaction sociale entre les modèles leaders.

de.title: SocialOmni: Bewertung der audiovisuellen sozialen Interaktivität in omnidirektionalen Modellen
de.summary: SocialOmni bewertet, wie gut multimodale LLMs die Gesprächsinteraktion über Sprechererkennung, Unterbruchstiming und Generierung hinweg bewältigen, und offenbaren signifikante Unterschiede in den Fähigkeiten zur sozialen Interaktion zwischen führenden Modellen.

es.title: SocialOmni: Evaluación comparativa de la interactividad social audiovisual en modelos omnidireccionales
es.summary: SocialOmni compara cómo los modelos de lenguaje multimodales manejan la interacción conversacional en la identificación del hablante, el tiempo de interrupción y generación, revelando una varianza significativa en las capacidades de interacción social entre los modelos líderes.

title: One Operator to Rule Them All? On Boundary-Indexed Operator Families in Neural PDE Solvers
summary: Research shows neural PDE solvers implicitly learn boundary-specific operators rather than universal ones, failing to generalize across different boundary condition distributions—a fundamental limitation of current approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.01406v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Neural PDE solvers are often described as learning solution operators that map problem data to PDE solutions. In this work, we argue that this interpretation is generally incorrect when boundary conditions vary. We show that standard neural operator training implicitly learns a boundary-indexed family of operators, rather than a single boundary-agnostic operator, with the learned mapping fundamentally conditioned on the boundary-condition distribution seen during training. We formalize this perspective by framing operator learning as conditional risk minimization over boundary conditions, which leads to a non-identifiability result outside the support of the training boundary distribution. As a consequence, generalization in forcing terms or resolution does not imply generalization across boundary conditions. We support our theoretical analysis with controlled experiments on the Poisson equation, demonstrating sharp degradation under boundary-condition shifts, cross-distribution failures between distinct boundary ensembles, and convergence to conditional expectations when boundary information is removed. Our results clarify a core limitation of current neural PDE solvers and highlight the need for explicit boundary-aware modeling in the pursuit of foundation models for PDEs.

zh.title: 一个算子统治所有？论神经偏微分方程求解器中的边界索引算子族
zh.summary: 研究表明神经偏微分方程求解器隐式学习特定边界的算子而非通用算子，在不同边界条件分布上泛化失败——这是当前方法的根本限制。

fr.title: Un opérateur pour les gouverner tous ? Sur les familles d'opérateurs indexées par les limites dans les solveurs de PDE neurales
fr.summary: La recherche montre que les solveurs de PDE neurales apprennent implicitement des opérateurs spécifiques aux limites plutôt que des opérateurs universels, échouant à généraliser sur différentes distributions de conditions limites—une limitation fondamentale des approches actuelles.

de.title: Ein Operator, sie alle zu beherrschen? Über grenzindexierte Operatorfamilien in neuronalen PDE-Lösern
de.summary: Die Forschung zeigt, dass neuronale PDE-Löser implizit grenzspezifische Operatoren anstelle von universellen Operatoren erlernen und nicht über verschiedene Randbedingungsverteilungen verallgemeinern können—eine grundlegende Einschränkung der gegenwärtigen Ansätze.

es.title: ¿Un operador para gobernarlos a todos? Sobre familias de operadores indexadas por límites en solucionadores PDE neurales
es.summary: La investigación muestra que los solucionadores de PDE neurales aprenden implícitamente operadores específicos de límites en lugar de universales, fallando en generalizar en diferentes distribuciones de condiciones de límites—una limitación fundamental de los enfoques actuales.

title: SAC-NeRF: Adaptive Ray Sampling for Neural Radiance Fields via Soft Actor-Critic Reinforcement Learning
summary: SAC-NeRF applies reinforcement learning to optimize ray sampling in Neural Radiance Fields, reducing sampling points by 35-48% while maintaining rendering quality through learned adaptive sampling strategies.
content:
arXiv:2603.15622v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) have achieved photorealistic novel view synthesis but suffer from computational inefficiency due to dense ray sampling during volume rendering. We propose SAC-NeRF, a reinforcement learning framework that learns adaptive sampling policies using Soft Actor-Critic (SAC). Our method formulates sampling as a Markov Decision Process where an RL agent learns to allocate samples based on scene characteristics. We introduce three technical components: (1) a Gaussian mixture distribution color model providing uncertainty estimates, (2) a multi-component reward function balancing quality, efficiency, and consistency, and (3) a two-stage training strategy addressing environment non-stationarity. Experiments on Synthetic-NeRF and LLFF datasets show that SAC-NeRF reduces sampling points by 35-48\% while maintaining rendering quality within 0.3-0.8 dB PSNR of dense sampling baselines. While the learned policy is scene-specific and the RL framework adds complexity compared to simpler heuristics, our work demonstrates that data-driven sampling strategies can discover effective patterns that would be difficult to hand-design.

zh.title: SAC-NeRF：通过柔性演员-评论家强化学习进行自适应射线采样的神经辐射场
zh.summary: SAC-NeRF应用强化学习优化神经辐射场中的射线采样，在保持渲染质量的同时，通过学习的自适应采样策略将采样点数减少了35-48%。

fr.title: SAC-NeRF : Échantillonnage de rayons adaptatif pour les champs de rayonnement neuraux via apprentissage par renforcement Soft Actor-Critic
fr.summary: SAC-NeRF applique l'apprentissage par renforcement pour optimiser l'échantillonnage des rayons dans les champs de rayonnement neuraux, réduisant les points d'échantillonnage de 35-48% tout en maintenant la qualité de rendu grâce à des stratégies d'échantillonnage adaptatif apprises.

de.title: SAC-NeRF: Adaptive Ray-Sampling für neuronale Strahlungsfelder via Soft Actor-Critic-Verstärkungslernen
de.summary: SAC-NeRF wendet Verstärkungslernen an, um das Ray-Sampling in neuronalen Strahlungsfeldern zu optimieren, wodurch die Abtastpunkte um 35-48% reduziert werden, während die Renderqualität durch gelernte adaptive Abtaststrategien erhalten bleibt.

es.title: SAC-NeRF: Muestreo de rayos adaptativo para campos de radiancia neurales mediante aprendizaje por refuerzo Soft Actor-Critic
es.summary: SAC-NeRF aplica aprendizaje por refuerzo para optimizar el muestreo de rayos en campos de radiancia neurales, reduciendo los puntos de muestreo en un 35-48% mientras se mantiene la calidad de renderizado a través de estrategias de muestreo adaptativo aprendidas.

title: Finder: A Multimodal AI-Powered Search Framework for Pharmaceutical Data Retrieval
summary: Finder is an AI-powered search framework unifying retrieval across text, images, audio, and video for pharmaceutical data using hybrid vector search, having processed over 291,400 documents in 98 languages.
content:
arXiv:2603.15623v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: AI is transforming pharmaceutical search, where traditional systems struggle with multimodal content and manual curation. Finder is a scalable AI-powered framework that unifies retrieval across text, images, audio, and video using hybrid vector search, combining sparse lexical and dense semantic models. Its modular pipeline ingests diverse formats, enriches metadata, and stores content in a vector-native backend. Finder supports reasoning-aware natural language search, improving precision and contextual relevance. The system has processed over 291,400 documents, 31,070 videos, and 1,192 audio files in 98 languages. Techniques like hybrid fusion, chunking, and metadata-aware routing enable intelligent access across regulatory, research, and commercial domains.

zh.title: Finder：用于药学数据检索的多模态人工智能驱动搜索框架
zh.summary: Finder是一个统一跨文本、图像、音频和视频的检索的人工智能搜索框架，用于药学数据，采用混合向量搜索，已处理超过291,400份文档，涵盖98种语言。

fr.title: Finder : Framework de recherche multimodal alimenté par l'IA pour la récupération de données pharmaceutiques
fr.summary: Finder est un framework de recherche alimenté par l'IA unifiant la récupération sur le texte, les images, l'audio et la vidéo pour les données pharmaceutiques en utilisant la recherche vectorielle hybride, ayant traité plus de 291 400 documents en 98 langues.

de.title: Finder: Ein multimodales KI-gestütztes Suchframework für den Abruf pharmazeutischer Daten
de.summary: Finder ist ein KI-gestütztes Such-Framework, das den Abruf über Text, Bilder, Audio und Video für pharmazeutische Daten mit Hybrid-Vektorsuche vereinheitlicht und über 291.400 Dokumente in 98 Sprachen verarbeitet hat.

es.title: Finder: Marco de búsqueda multimodal impulsado por IA para recuperación de datos farmacéuticos
es.summary: Finder es un marco de búsqueda impulsado por IA que unifica la recuperación en texto, imágenes, audio y video para datos farmacéuticos utilizando búsqueda vectorial híbrida, habiendo procesado más de 291.400 documentos en 98 idiomas.

title: Exploring the Use of VLMs for Navigation Assistance for People with Blindness and Low Vision
summary: A comprehensive evaluation shows GPT-4o performs best for navigation assistance to visually impaired users, but open-source models struggle with spatial reasoning—highlighting the need for better accessibility-focused alignment.
content:
arXiv:2603.15624v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper investigates the potential of vision-language models (VLMs) to assist people with blindness and low vision (pBLV) in navigation tasks. We evaluate state-of-the-art closed-source models, including GPT-4V, GPT-4o, Gemini-1.5-Pro, and Claude-3.5-Sonnet, alongside open-source models, such as Llava-v1.6-mistral and Llava-onevision-qwen, to analyze their capabilities in foundational visual skills: counting ambient obstacles, relative spatial reasoning, and common-sense wayfinding-pertinent scene understanding. We further assess their performance in navigation scenarios, using pBLV-specific prompts designed to simulate real-world assistance tasks. Our findings reveal notable performance disparities between these models: GPT-4o consistently outperforms others across all tasks, particularly in spatial reasoning and scene understanding. In contrast, open-source models struggle with nuanced reasoning and adaptability in complex environments. Common challenges include difficulties in accurately counting objects in cluttered settings, biases in spatial reasoning, and a tendency to prioritize object details over spatial feedback, limiting their usability for pBLV in navigation tasks. Despite these limitations, VLMs show promise for wayfinding assistance when better aligned with human feedback and equipped with improved spatial reasoning. This research provides actionable insights into the strengths and limitations of current VLMs, guiding developers on effectively integrating VLMs into assistive technologies while addressing key limitations for enhanced usability.

zh.title: 探索视觉语言模型在盲人和低视力人群导航辅助中的应用
zh.summary: 一项综合评估表明，GPT-4o在为视觉障碍用户提供导航辅助方面表现最佳，但开源模型在空间推理方面表现不佳，突出了改进无障碍对齐的必要性。

fr.title: Exploration de l'utilisation des VLM pour l'assistance à la navigation pour les personnes aveugles et malvoyantes
fr.summary: Une évaluation complète montre que GPT-4o fonctionne mieux pour l'assistance à la navigation des utilisateurs malvoyants, mais les modèles open-source peinent à raisonner sur l'espace, soulignant le besoin d'un meilleur alignement axé sur l'accessibilité.

de.title: Erkundung der Verwendung von VLMs für Navigationsassistenz für Menschen mit Blindheit und Sehbehinderung
de.summary: Eine umfassende Bewertung zeigt, dass GPT-4o am besten für die Navigationsunterstützung sehbehinderter Benutzer funktioniert, aber Open-Source-Modelle haben Schwierigkeiten mit räumlichem Denken – was die Notwendigkeit einer besseren Ausrichtung auf Barrierefreiheit unterstreicht.

es.title: Exploración del uso de VLM para asistencia en navegación para personas ciegas y con baja visión
es.summary: Una evaluación exhaustiva muestra que GPT-4o funciona mejor para la asistencia en navegación de usuarios con discapacidad visual, pero los modelos de código abierto luchan con el razonamiento espacial, lo que destaca la necesidad de una alineación mejor enfocada en accesibilidad.

title: XLinear: Frequency-Enhanced MLP with CrossFilter for Robust Long-Range Forecasting
summary: XLinear combines frequency-domain operations with multi-layer perceptrons for time series forecasting, decomposing data into trend and seasonal components to achieve state-of-the-art performance while maintaining robustness to noise.
content:
arXiv:2603.15645v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Time series forecasters are widely used across various domains. Among them, MLP (multi-layer perceptron)-based forecasters have been proven to be more robust to noise compared to Transformer-based forecasters. However, MLP struggles to capture complex features, resulting in limitations on capturing long-range dependencies. To address this challenge, we propose XLinear, an MLP-based forecaster for long-range forecasting. Firstly, we decompose the time series into trend and seasonal components. For the trend component which contains long-range characteristics, we design Enhanced Frequency Attention (EFA) to capture long-term dependencies by leveraging frequency-domain operations. Additionally, a CrossFilter Block is proposed for the seasonal component to maintain the model's robustness to noise, avoiding the problems of low robustness often caused by attention mechanisms. Experimental results demonstrate that XLinear achieves state-of-the-art performance on test datasets. While keeping the lightweight architecture and high robustness of MLP-based models, our forecaster outperforms other MLP-based forecasters in capturing long-range dependencies.

zh.title: XLinear：具有CrossFilter的频率增强多层感知器用于稳健的长期预测
zh.summary: XLinear将频域操作与多层感知器相结合用于时间序列预测，将数据分解为趋势和季节性成分，以实现最先进的性能，同时保持对噪声的鲁棒性。

fr.title: XLinear : MLP amélioré en fréquence avec CrossFilter pour la prévision robuste à long terme
fr.summary: XLinear combine les opérations dans le domaine fréquentiel avec les perceptrons multicouches pour la prévision des séries chronologiques, décomposant les données en composantes tendance et saisonnière pour atteindre des performances à la pointe de la technologie tout en maintenant la robustesse au bruit.

de.title: XLinear: Frequenzverstärktes MLP mit CrossFilter für robuste Langzeitprognosen
de.summary: XLinear kombiniert Frequenzbereichsoperationen mit mehrschichtigen Perceptronen für Zeitreihenprognosen, zerlegt Daten in Trend- und Saisonkomponenten und erreicht modernste Leistung bei gleichzeitiger Robustheit gegen Rauschen.

es.title: XLinear: MLP mejorado con frecuencia con CrossFilter para pronósticos robustos a largo plazo
es.summary: XLinear combina operaciones en el dominio de la frecuencia con perceptrones multicapa para el pronóstico de series temporales, descomponiendo datos en componentes de tendencia y estacionales para lograr un rendimiento de última generación manteniendo la robustez al ruido.

title: Alternating Reinforcement Learning with Contextual Rubric Rewards
summary: Researchers propose ARL-RR, a reinforcement learning framework that eliminates scalar reward aggregation by optimizing one rubric dimension at a time. Tests on HealthBench demonstrate consistent performance improvements across model scales without fixed weighting constraints.
content:
arXiv:2603.15646v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Rubric Rewards (RLRR) is a framework that extends conventional reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and verifiable rewards (RLVR) by replacing scalar preference signals with structured, multi-dimensional, contextual rubric-based evaluations. However, existing approaches in RLRR are limited to linearly compressing vector rewards into a scalar reward with a fixed weightings, which is sensitive to artificial score design and fails to capture correlations among reward dimensions. To overcome the limitations of reward aggregation, this work proposes Alternating Reinforcement Learning with Rubric Rewards (ARL-RR), a framework that eliminates the need for a fixed scalarization by optimizing one semantic rubric meta-class at a time. Theoretically, we show that reward aggregation induces a variance contraction effect, which helps explain the performance gains. We further introduce a lightweight, search-based adaptation procedure that selects the next meta-class dynamically based on task performance, enabling the policy to emphasize critical objectives and thereby improve the model performance. Empirically, our experiments on the HealthBench dataset with experts annotations demonstrate that ARL-RR uniformly outperforms scalarized methods in both model performance and training efficiency across different model scales (1.7B, 4B, 8B, and 14B).

zh.title: 具有情境评分表奖励的交替强化学习
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了ARL-RR，一个强化学习框架，通过一次优化一个评分表维度来消除标量奖励聚合。HealthBench上的测试证明了在没有固定权重约束的情况下，在各种模型规模上的一致性能改进。

fr.title: Apprentissage par Renforcement Alterné avec Récompenses de Rubrique Contextuelle
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent ARL-RR, un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement qui élimine l'agrégation de récompenses scalaires en optimisant une dimension de rubrique à la fois. Les tests sur HealthBench démontrent des améliorations de performance cohérentes sur les échelles de modèles sans contraintes de pondération fixes.

de.title: Alternierendes Reinforcement Learning mit kontextuellen Rubrik-Belohnungen
de.summary: Forscher schlagen ARL-RR vor, ein Reinforcement-Learning-Framework, das die skalare Belohnungsaggregation durch Optimierung einer Rubrik-Dimension auf einmal eliminiert. Tests auf HealthBench zeigen konsistente Leistungsverbesserungen über Modellskalen hinweg ohne feste Gewichtungsbeschränkungen.

es.title: Aprendizaje Reforzado Alterno con Recompensas de Rúbrica Contextual
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen ARL-RR, un marco de aprendizaje reforzado que elimina la agregación de recompensas escalares optimizando una dimensión de rúbrica a la vez. Las pruebas en HealthBench demuestran mejoras de rendimiento consistentes en escalas de modelos sin restricciones de ponderación fijas.

title: Steering Frozen LLMs: Adaptive Social Alignment via Online Prompt Routing
summary: CCLUB framework enables adaptive social alignment through system-prompt routing without model retraining, defending against evolving jailbreak behaviors. The approach achieves 10.98% improvement in cumulative reward by using conservative consensus clustering to prevent unsafe generalization.
content:
arXiv:2603.15647v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are typically governed by post-training alignment (e.g., RLHF or DPO), which yields a largely static policy during deployment and inference. However, real-world safety is a full-lifecycle problem: static defenses degrade against evolving jailbreak behaviors, and fixed weights cannot adapt to pluralistic, time-varying safety norms. This motivates inference-time governance that steers behavior without costly retraining. To address this, we introduce the Consensus Clustering LinUCB Bandit (CCLUB), a unified framework for adaptive social alignment via system-prompt routing. CCLUB employs a conservative consensus clustering mechanism: it pools data only within the intersection of utility and safety similarity graphs, effectively preventing unsafe generalization across semantically proximal but risk-divergent contexts. Our theoretical analysis yields a sublinear regret guarantee, demonstrating near-optimal performance of CCLUB. Extensive experiments validate that CCLUB outperforms strong baselines, achieving a 10.98% improvement in cumulative reward and a 14.42% reduction in the average suboptimality gap.

zh.title: 引导冻结的大语言模型：通过在线提示路由的自适应社会对齐
zh.summary: CCLUB框架通过系统提示路由实现自适应社会对齐，无需模型重新训练，防止不断演变的越狱行为。该方法通过使用保守的共识聚类来防止不安全的泛化，在累积奖励中实现了10.98%的改进。

fr.title: Diriger les LLM Gelés : Alignement Social Adaptatif via Routage de Prompt en Ligne
fr.summary: Le cadre CCLUB permet un alignement social adaptatif grâce à l'acheminement des invites système sans recyclage du modèle, se défendant contre les comportements de contournement en évolution. L'approche réalise une amélioration de 10,98% de la récompense cumulative en utilisant un clustering de consensus conservateur pour prévenir la généralisation non sécurisée.

de.title: Steuerung gefrorener LLMs: Adaptive soziale Ausrichtung durch Online-Prompt-Routing
de.summary: Das CCLUB-Framework ermöglicht adaptive soziale Ausrichtung durch System-Prompt-Routing ohne Modellumschulung und wehrt sich gegen sich entwickelnde Jailbreak-Verhaltensweisen. Der Ansatz erreicht eine Verbesserung von 10,98% in der kumulativen Belohnung durch die Verwendung von konservativer Konsens-Clustering, um unsichere Verallgemeinerung zu verhindern.

es.title: Dirección de LLM Congelados: Alineación Social Adaptativa a través de Enrutamiento de Prompts en Línea
es.summary: El marco CCLUB permite alineación social adaptativa a través del enrutamiento de prompts del sistema sin reentrenamiento del modelo, defendiéndose contra comportamientos de jailbreak en evolución. El enfoque logra una mejora del 10,98% en la recompensa acumulada utilizando agrupamiento de consenso conservador para prevenir la generalización insegura.

title: A federated learning framework with knowledge graph and temporal transformer for early sepsis prediction in multi-center ICUs
summary: A privacy-preserving federated learning framework combines knowledge graphs and temporal transformers to predict sepsis across hospitals without sharing raw patient data. The approach achieves 95.6% AUC, significantly outperforming both centralized and standard federated methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.15651v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The early prediction of sepsis in intensive care unit (ICU) patients is crucial for improving survival rates. However, the development of accurate predictive models is hampered by data fragmentation across healthcare institutions and the complex, temporal nature of medical data, all under stringent privacy constraints. To address these challenges, we propose a novel framework that uniquely integrates federated learning (FL) with a medical knowledge graph and a temporal transformer model, enhanced by meta-learning capabilities. Our approach enables collaborative model training across multiple hospitals without sharing raw patient data, thereby preserving privacy. The model leverages a knowledge graph to incorporate structured medical relationships and employs a temporal transformer to capture long-range dependencies in clinical time-series data. A model-agnostic meta-learning (MAML) strategy is further incorporated to facilitate rapid adaptation of the global model to local data distributions. Evaluated on the MIMIC-IV and eICU datasets, our method achieves an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.956, which represents a 22.4% improvement over conventional centralized models and a 12.7% improvement over standard federated learning, demonstrating strong predictive capability for sepsis. This work presents a reliable and privacy-preserving solution for multi-center collaborative early warning of sepsis.

zh.title: 用知识图谱和时间变换器的联邦学习框架用于多中心ICU的早期脓毒症预测
zh.summary: 一个隐私保护的联邦学习框架结合知识图谱和时间变换器来预测跨医院的脓毒症，而无需共享原始患者数据。该方法达到95.6% AUC，明显优于集中式和标准联邦方法。

fr.title: Un cadre d'apprentissage fédéré avec graphe de connaissances et transformateur temporel pour la prédiction précoce de la septicémie en unités de soins intensifs multi-centres
fr.summary: Un cadre d'apprentissage fédéré préservant la confidentialité combine des graphes de connaissances et des transformateurs temporels pour prédire la septicémie entre les hôpitaux sans partager les données brutes des patients. L'approche atteint un AUC de 95,6%, surpassant nettement les méthodes centralisées et fédérées standards.

de.title: Ein föderiertes Lernrahmenwerk mit Wissensgraph und temporalem Transformer zur Früherkennung von Sepsis in Multi-Center-Intensivstationen
de.summary: Ein datenschutzgerechtes föderiertes Lernrahmenwerk kombiniert Wissensgraphen und temporale Transformer, um Sepsis in Krankenhäusern vorherzusagen, ohne rohe Patientendaten auszutauschen. Das Verfahren erreicht 95,6% AUC und übertrifft sowohl zentralisierte als auch standardmäßige föderierte Methoden deutlich.

es.title: Un marco de aprendizaje federado con gráfico de conocimiento y transformador temporal para la predicción temprana de sepsis en UCI multi-centro
es.summary: Un marco de aprendizaje federado que preserva la privacidad combina gráficos de conocimiento y transformadores temporales para predecir sepsis en hospitales sin compartir datos sin procesar de pacientes. El enfoque logra un AUC de 95,6%, superando significativamente tanto los métodos centralizados como los federados estándar.

title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
summary: SRLM framework uses self-reflection to select context-interaction programs more effectively than recursive approaches, with up to 22% improvement on long-context tasks. Self-consistency, reasoning length, and verbalized confidence serve as reliable uncertainty indicators for program selection.
content:
arXiv:2603.15653v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Long-context handling remains a core challenge for language models: even with extended context windows, models often fail to reliably extract, reason over, and use the information across long contexts. Recent works like Recursive Language Models (RLM) have approached this challenge by agentic way of decomposing long contexts into recursive sub-calls through programmatic interaction at inference. While promising, the success of RLM critically depends on how these context-interaction programs are selected, which has remained largely unexplored. In this paper, we study this problem and introduce SRLM, a framework that augments programmatic context interaction with uncertainty-aware Self-Reflection. SRLM leverages three intrinsic signals: self consistency, reasoning length, and verbalized confidence. These serve as complementary indicators of a model's internal uncertainty, and the model uses them to evaluate and compare candidate context-interaction programs. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmark datasets, context lengths, and backbone models, show that SRLM consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, yielding up to 22% improvement over RLM under the same time budget. Our findings show that recursion itself is not the primary driver of performance in RLM, and a simple self-reflective program search can match or surpass RLM without requiring self-query or explicit recursion mechanisms. We find that for context lengths within the model's window, RLMs with recursion often degrade performance relative to the base model, whereas SRLM yields consistent gains across both short and long contexts. We also find that RLM is less effective in tasks with semantically intensive nature, where heuristic program search is insufficient and broader contextual understanding is required, while self-reflection in SRLM provides a semantic signal that better steers reasoning in these scenarios.

zh.title: 递归语言模型遇到不确定性：自反思程序搜索在长上下文中的惊人有效性
zh.summary: SRLM框架使用自反思来比递归方法更有效地选择上下文交互程序，在长上下文任务上改进高达22%。自一致性、推理长度和言语化置信度作为程序选择的可靠不确定性指标。

fr.title: Les modèles de langage récursifs rencontrent l'incertitude : L'efficacité surprenante de la recherche de programme auto-réfléchie pour le contexte long
fr.summary: Le cadre SRLM utilise l'auto-réflexion pour sélectionner plus efficacement les programmes d'interaction contextuelle que les approches récursives, avec une amélioration allant jusqu'à 22% sur les tâches de contexte long. L'auto-cohérence, la longueur du raisonnement et la confiance verbalisée servent d'indicateurs d'incertitude fiables pour la sélection des programmes.

de.title: Rekursive Sprachmodelle treffen auf Unsicherheit: Die überraschende Effektivität der selbstreflektiven Programmsuche für langfristigen Kontext
de.summary: Das SRLM-Framework nutzt Selbstreflexion, um Kontext-Interaktionsprogramme effektiver auszuwählen als rekursive Ansätze, mit bis zu 22% Verbesserung bei Aufgaben mit langem Kontext. Selbstkonsistenz, Argumentationslänge und verbalisiertes Vertrauen dienen als zuverlässige Unsicherheitsindikatoren für die Programmauswahl.

es.title: Modelos de lenguaje recursivos se encuentran con incertidumbre: La sorprendente efectividad de la búsqueda de programas auto-reflexivos para contexto largo
es.summary: El marco SRLM utiliza la autorreflexión para seleccionar programas de interacción contextual de manera más efectiva que los enfoques recursivos, con una mejora de hasta el 22% en tareas de contexto largo. La autoconsistencia, la longitud del razonamiento y la confianza verbalizada sirven como indicadores de incertidumbre confiables para la selección de programas.

title: Discovering the Hidden Role of Gini Index In Prompt-based Classification
summary: Research reveals the Gini Index as a metric for detecting and correcting accuracy disparities in prompt-based classification, particularly for underperforming minority classes. A post-hoc debiasing method significantly reduces relative and absolute accuracy imbalances across classification tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.15654v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: In classification tasks, the long-tailed minority classes usually offer the predictions that are most important. Yet these classes consistently exhibit low accuracies, whereas a few high-performing classes dominate the game. We pursue a foundational understanding of the hidden role of Gini Index as a tool for detecting and optimizing (debiasing) disparities in class accuracy, focusing on the case of prompt-based classification. We introduce the intuitions, benchmark Gini scores in real-world LLMs and vision models, and thoroughly discuss the insights of Gini not only as a measure of relative accuracy dominance but also as a direct optimization metric. Through rigorous case analyses, we first show that weak to strong relative accuracy imbalance exists in both prompt-based, text and image classification results and regardless of whether the classification is high-dimensional or low-dimensional. Then, we harness the Gini metric to propose a post-hoc model-agnostic bias mitigation method. Experimental results across few-shot news, biomedical, and zero-shot image classification show that our method significantly reduces both relative and absolute accuracy imbalances, minimizing top class relative dominance while elevating weakest classes.

zh.title: 发现基尼指数在基于提示的分类中的隐藏角色
zh.summary: 研究揭示了基尼指数作为检测和纠正基于提示的分类中准确性差异的指标，特别是对于表现不佳的少数类。后处理去偏方法显著减少了跨分类任务的相对和绝对准确性失衡。

fr.title: Découvrir le rôle caché de l'indice de Gini dans la classification basée sur les invites
fr.summary: La recherche révèle l'indice de Gini comme une métrique pour détecter et corriger les disparités de précision dans la classification basée sur les invites, en particulier pour les classes minoritaires peu performantes. Une méthode de débiaisage post-hoc réduit considérablement les déséquilibres de précision relatifs et absolus entre les tâches de classification.

de.title: Die versteckte Rolle des Gini-Index bei der aufforderungsgestützten Klassifizierung entdecken
de.summary: Die Forschung offenbart den Gini-Index als Metrik zur Erkennung und Behebung von Genauigkeitsunterschieden bei der aufforderungsgestützten Klassifizierung, insbesondere bei unterperformanten Minderheitsklassen. Eine nachträgliche Debias-Methode reduziert erheblich relative und absolute Genauigkeitsungleichgewichte bei Klassifizierungsaufgaben.

es.title: Descubriendo el papel oculto del índice de Gini en la clasificación basada en indicaciones
es.summary: La investigación revela el índice de Gini como una métrica para detectar y corregir disparidades de precisión en la clasificación basada en indicaciones, particularmente para clases minoritarias con bajo desempeño. Un método de desviación post-hoc reduce significativamente los desequilibrios de precisión relativos y absolutos en todas las tareas de clasificación.

title: Beyond Reward Suppression: Reshaping Steganographic Communication Protocols in MARL via Dynamic Representational Circuit Breaking
summary: DRCB introduces an architectural defense against steganographic collusion in multi-agent RL by monitoring representational dynamics through a VQ-VAE bottleneck. The system prevents hidden communication protocols while maintaining cooperative performance across agents.
content:
arXiv:2603.15655v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: In decentralized Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL), steganographic collusion -- where agents develop private protocols to evade monitoring -- presents a critical AI safety threat. Existing defenses, limited to behavioral or reward layers, fail to detect coordination in latent communication channels. We introduce the Dynamic Representational Circuit Breaker (DRCB), an architectural defense operating at the optimization substrate.
  Building on the AI Mother Tongue (AIM) framework, DRCB utilizes a Vector Quantized Variational Autoencoder (VQ-VAE) bottleneck to convert unobservable messages into auditable statistical objects. DRCB monitors signals including Jensen-Shannon Divergence drift, L2-norm codebook displacement, and Randomized Observer Pool accuracy to compute an EMA-based Collusion Score. Threshold breaches trigger four escalating interventions: dynamic adaptation, gradient-space penalty injection into the Advantage function A^pi, temporal reward suppression, and full substrate circuit breaking via codebook shuffling and optimizer state reset.
  Experiments on a Contextual Prisoner's Dilemma with MNIST labels show that while static monitoring fails (p = 0.3517), DRCB improves observer mean accuracy from 0.858 to 0.938 (+9.3 percent) and reduces volatility by 43 percent, while preserving mean joint reward (p = 0.854). Analysis of 214,298 symbol samples confirms "Semantic Degradation," where high-frequency sequences converge to zero entropy, foreclosing complex steganographic encodings. We identify a "Transparency Paradox" where agents achieve surface-level determinism while preserving residual capacity in long-tail distributions, reflecting Goodhart's Law. This task-agnostic methodology provides a technical path toward MICA-compliant (Multi-Agent Internal Coupling Audit) pre-deployment auditing for autonomous systems.

zh.title: 超越奖励抑制：通过动态表示断路器在MARL中重塑隐写通信协议
zh.summary: DRCB通过VQ-VAE瓶颈监测表示动态，为多智能体强化学习中的隐写勾结引入架构防御。该系统防止隐藏的通信协议，同时保持智能体间的合作性能。

fr.title: Au-delà de la suppression des récompenses : Remodeler les protocoles de communication stéganographiques dans MARL via le circuit-breaking représentationnel dynamique
fr.summary: DRCB introduit une défense architecturale contre la collusion stéganographique dans l'apprentissage par renforcement multi-agents en surveillant la dynamique représentationnelle via un goulot d'étranglement VQ-VAE. Le système empêche les protocoles de communication cachés tout en maintenant les performances coopératives entre agents.

de.title: Jenseits der Belohnungsunterdrückung: Umgestaltung stéganographischer Kommunikationsprotokolle in MARL durch dynamische Darstellungsunterbrecher
de.summary: DRCB führt eine architektonische Verteidigung gegen stéganographische Kollusion in Multi-Agent-RL ein, indem die Darstellungsdynamik durch einen VQ-VAE-Engpass überwacht wird. Das System verhindert versteckte Kommunikationsprotokolle und erhält gleichzeitig die kooperative Leistung zwischen Agenten.

es.title: Más allá de la supresión de recompensas: Remodelación de protocolos de comunicación esteganográfica en MARL mediante interrupciones de circuitos representacionales dinámicos
es.summary: DRCB introduce una defensa arquitectónica contra la colusión esteganográfica en RL multiagente monitoreando la dinámica representacional a través de un cuello de botella VQ-VAE. El sistema previene protocolos de comunicación ocultos mientras mantiene el desempeño cooperativo entre agentes.

title: Attribution-Guided Model Rectification of Unreliable Neural Network Behaviors
summary: A rank-one model editing framework uses attribution analysis to identify and fix unreliable neural network behaviors without expensive retraining. The method effectively corrects neural trojans and spurious correlations using minimal cleansed samples.
content:
arXiv:2603.15656v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The performance of neural network models deteriorates due to their unreliable behavior on non-robust features of corrupted samples. Owing to their opaque nature, rectifying models to address this problem often necessitates arduous data cleaning and model retraining, resulting in huge computational and manual overhead. In this work, we leverage rank-one model editing to establish an attribution-guided model rectification framework that effectively locates and corrects model unreliable behaviors. We first distinguish our rectification setting from existing model editing, yielding a formulation that corrects unreliable behavior while preserving model performance and reducing reliance on large budgets of cleansed samples. We further reveal a bottleneck of model rectifying arising from heterogeneous editability across layers. To target the primary source of misbehavior, we introduce an attribution-guided layer localization method that quantifies layer-wise editability and identifies the layer most responsible for unreliabilities. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in correcting unreliabilities observed for neural Trojans, spurious correlations and feature leakage. Our method shows remarkable performance by achieving its editing objective with as few as a single cleansed sample, which makes it appealing for practice.

zh.title: 归因引导的模型修正：不可靠神经网络行为的纠正
zh.summary: 一个秩一模型编辑框架使用归因分析来识别和修复不可靠的神经网络行为，无需昂贵的重新训练。该方法使用最少的清洁样本有效地纠正神经木马和虚假相关性。

fr.title: Rectification de modèles guidée par l'attribution des comportements de réseau neuronal peu fiables
fr.summary: Un cadre d'édition de modèles de rang un utilise l'analyse d'attribution pour identifier et corriger les comportements de réseau neuronal peu fiables sans réentraînement coûteux. La méthode corrige efficacement les chevaux de Troie neuronaux et les corrélations fallacieuses en utilisant un nombre minimal d'échantillons nettoyés.

de.title: Attributionsgesteuerte Modellberichtigung unzuverlässigen neuronalen Netzwerkverhaltens
de.summary: Ein Rang-Eins-Modellbearbeitungsrahmen verwendet Attributionsanalyse, um unzuverlässiges neuronales Netzwerkverhalten ohne teure Umschulung zu identifizieren und zu beheben. Die Methode korrigiert effektiv neuronale Trojaner und spurlose Korrelationen mit minimalen bereinigten Proben.

es.title: Rectificación de modelos guiada por atribución de comportamientos de redes neuronales no confiables
es.summary: Un marco de edición de modelos de rango uno utiliza análisis de atribución para identificar y corregir comportamientos de redes neuronales no confiables sin reentrenamiento costoso. El método corrige efectivamente los troyanos neuronales y las correlaciones espurias utilizando muestras limpias mínimas.

title: OrthoAI v2: From Single-Agent Segmentation to Dual-Agent Treatment Planning for Clear Aligners
summary: OrthoAI v2 expands orthodontic treatment planning with dual agents for segmentation and landmark detection, paired with a comprehensive six-category biomechanical scoring model. Performance improvements of 21% over v1 are maintained on commodity CPUs with full CPU deployability.
content:
arXiv:2603.15663v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present OrthoAI v2, the second iteration of our open-source pipeline for AI-assisted orthodontic treatment planning with clear aligners, substantially extending the single-agent framework previously introduced. The first version established a proof-of-concept based on Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (\dgcnn{}) for tooth segmentation but was limited to per-tooth centroid extraction, lacked landmark-level precision, and produced a scalar quality score without staging simulation. \vtwo{} addresses all three limitations through three principal contributions: (i)~a second agent adopting the Conditioned Heatmap Regression Methodology (\charm{})~\cite{rodriguez2025charm} for direct, segmentation-free dental landmark detection, fused with Agent~1 via a confidence-weighted orchestrator in three modes (parallel, sequential, single-agent); (ii)~a composite six-category biomechanical scoring model (biomechanics $\times$ 0.30 + staging $\times$ 0.20 + attachments $\times$ 0.15 + IPR $\times$ 0.10 + occlusion $\times$ 0.10 + predictability $\times$ 0.15) replacing the binary pass/fail check of v1; (iii)~a multi-frame treatment simulator generating $F = A \times r$ temporally coherent 6-DoF tooth trajectories via SLERP interpolation and evidence-based staging rules, enabling ClinCheck 4D visualisation. On a synthetic benchmark of 200 crowding scenarios, the parallel ensemble of OrthoAI v2 reaches a planning quality score of $92.8 \pm 4.1$ vs.\ $76.4 \pm 8.3$ for OrthoAI v1, a $+21\%$ relative gain, while maintaining full CPU deployability ($4.2 \pm 0.8$~s).

zh.title: OrthoAI v2：从单智能体分割到隐形牙套的双智能体治疗规划
zh.summary: OrthoAI v2通过双智能体进行分割和地标检测扩展正畸治疗规划，配合全面的六类生物力学评分模型。与v1相比性能提高21%，在商用CPU上保持，完全支持CPU部署。

fr.title: OrthoAI v2 : De la segmentation monoagent à la planification du traitement biagent pour les gouttières invisibles
fr.summary: OrthoAI v2 étend la planification du traitement orthodontique avec des agents doubles pour la segmentation et la détection de points de repère, associés à un modèle complet de notation biomécanique à six catégories. Les améliorations de performance de 21% par rapport à v1 sont maintenues sur les processeurs de base avec déploiement CPU complet.

de.title: OrthoAI v2: Von der Einzelagenten-Segmentierung zur Dual-Agent-Behandlungsplanung für transparente Zahnspangen
de.summary: OrthoAI v2 erweitert die orthodontische Behandlungsplanung mit dualen Agenten für Segmentierung und Landmarkenerkennung, gepaart mit einem umfassenden Bewertungsmodell für sechs Biomechanik-Kategorien. Leistungsverbesserungen von 21% gegenüber v1 werden auf Standard-CPUs beibehalten und sind vollständig CPU-einsetzbar.

es.title: OrthoAI v2: De la segmentación de un solo agente a la planificación del tratamiento de doble agente para alineadores transparentes
es.summary: OrthoAI v2 amplía la planificación del tratamiento ortodóncico con agentes duales para segmentación y detección de puntos de referencia, junto con un modelo integral de puntuación biomecánica de seis categorías. Las mejoras de rendimiento del 21% respecto a v1 se mantienen en CPU básicas con capacidad de implementación completa en CPU.

title: Quantum Amplitude Estimation for Catastrophe Insurance Tail-Risk Pricing: Empirical Convergence and NISQ Noise Analysis
summary: Quantum amplitude estimation achieves quadratic speedup over classical Monte Carlo for catastrophe insurance tail-risk pricing, enabling high-resolution loss distribution estimates. Empirical validation on real weather data (58,028 NOAA storm records) demonstrates oracle-model advantages.
content:
arXiv:2603.15664v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Classical Monte Carlo methods for pricing catastrophe insurance tail risk converge at order reciprocal root N, requiring large simulation budgets to resolve upper-tail percentiles of the loss distribution. This sample-sparsity problem can lead to AI models trained on impoverished tail data, producing poorly calibrated risk estimates where insolvency risk is greatest. Quantum Amplitude Estimation (QAE), following Montanaro, achieves convergence approaching order reciprocal N in oracle queries - a quadratic speedup that, at scale, would enable high-resolution tail estimation within practical budgets. We validate this advantage empirically using a Qiskit Aer simulator with genuine Grover amplification. A complete pipeline encodes fitted lognormal catastrophe distributions into quantum oracles via amplitude encoding, producing small readout probabilities that enable safe Grover amplification with up to k=16 iterations. Seven experiments on synthetic and real (NOAA Storm Events, 58,028 records) data yield three main findings: an oracle-model advantage, that strong classical baselines win when analytical access is available, and that discretisation, not estimation, is the current bottleneck.

zh.title: 用于巨灾保险尾部风险定价的量子振幅估计：经验收敛和NISQ噪声分析
zh.summary: 量子振幅估计相比经典蒙特卡洛方法在巨灾保险尾部风险定价中实现了二次加速，能够进行高分辨率损失分布估计。基于真实天气数据（58,028条美国国家气象局风暴记录）的经验验证证明了预言机模型的优势。

fr.title: Estimation d'amplitude quantique pour l'évaluation du risque de queue en assurance catastrophe : Convergence empirique et analyse du bruit NISQ
fr.summary: L'estimation d'amplitude quantique réalise une accélération quadratique par rapport à la méthode de Monte-Carlo classique pour l'évaluation du risque de queue en assurance catastrophe, permettant des estimations de distribution des pertes à haute résolution. La validation empirique sur des données météorologiques réelles (58 028 enregistrements de tempêtes du NOAA) démontre les avantages du modèle oracle.

de.title: Quantenampitudenschätzung zur Bewertung von Tail-Risk-Preisen in der Katastrophenversicherung: Empirische Konvergenz und NISQ-Rauschanalyse
de.summary: Die Quantenampitudenschätzung erreicht eine quadratische Beschleunigung gegenüber dem klassischen Monte-Carlo-Verfahren bei der Tail-Risk-Preisgestaltung in der Katastrophenversicherung und ermöglicht hochauflösende Schadensverteilungsschätzungen. Die empirische Validierung anhand echter Wetterdaten (58.028 NOAA-Sturmaufzeichnungen) demonstriert die Vorteile des Oracle-Modells.

es.title: Estimación de Amplitud Cuántica para Fijación de Precios de Riesgo de Cola en Seguros de Catástrofe: Convergencia Empírica y Análisis de Ruido NISQ
es.summary: La estimación de amplitud cuántica logra una aceleración cuadrática sobre el método clásico de Monte Carlo para la fijación de precios de riesgo de cola en seguros de catástrofe, lo que permite estimaciones de distribución de pérdidas de alta resolución. La validación empírica en datos meteorológicos reales (58.028 registros de tormentas del NOAA) demuestra las ventajas del modelo oracle.

title: DRCY: Agentic Hardware Design Reviews
summary: DRCY is a production-deployed multi-agent LLM system that automatically reviews hardware schematics by fetching datasheets and analyzing pin-by-pin connectivity against specifications. Currently used by major hardware companies for vehicle and space exploration design verification.
content:
arXiv:2603.15672v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Hardware design errors discovered after fabrication require costly physical respins that can delay products by months. Existing electronic design automation (EDA) tools enforce structural connectivity rules. However, they cannot verify that connections are \emph{semantically} correct with respect to component datasheets. For example, that a symbol's pinout matches the manufacturer's specification, or that a voltage regulator's feedback resistors produce the intended output. We present DRCY, the first production-ready multi-agent LLM system that automates first-pass schematic connection review by autonomously fetching component datasheets, performing pin-by-pin analysis against extracted specifications, and posting findings as inline comments on design reviews. DRCY is deployed in production on AllSpice Hub, a collaborative hardware design platform, where it runs as a CI/CD action triggered on design review submissions. DRCY is used regularly by major hardware companies for use-cases ranging from multi-agent vehicle design to space exploration. We describe DRCY's five-agent pipeline architecture, its agentic datasheet retrieval system with self-evaluation, and its multi-run consensus mechanism for improving reliability on safety-critical analyses

zh.title: DRCY：代理硬件设计审查
zh.summary: DRCY是一个生产部署的多代理LLM系统，通过获取数据表并针对规格进行逐引脚连接分析来自动审查硬件原理图。目前被主要硬件公司用于车辆和航天探索设计验证。

fr.title: DRCY : Revues de Conception Matérielle Agentic
fr.summary: DRCY est un système LLM multi-agents déployé en production qui examine automatiquement les schémas matériels en récupérant les fiches techniques et en analysant la connectivité broche par broche par rapport aux spécifications. Actuellement utilisé par les grandes entreprises de matériel informatique pour la vérification de la conception des véhicules et de l'exploration spatiale.

de.title: DRCY: Agentische Hardware-Designüberprüfungen
de.summary: DRCY ist ein in der Produktion eingesetztes Multi-Agent-LLM-System, das Hardware-Schaltpläne automatisch überprüft, indem es Datenblätter abruft und die Pin-für-Pin-Konnektivität gegen Spezifikationen analysiert. Wird derzeit von großen Hardwareunternehmen zur Designverifikation von Fahrzeugen und Weltraumforschung eingesetzt.

es.title: DRCY: Revisiones de Diseño de Hardware Agentivo
es.summary: DRCY es un sistema LLM multi-agente implementado en producción que revisa automáticamente esquemas de hardware obteniendo hojas de datos y analizando la conectividad pin por pin contra especificaciones. Actualmente utilizado por grandes empresas de hardware para verificación de diseño de vehículos y exploración espacial.

title: Automated Self-Testing as a Quality Gate: Evidence-Driven Release Management for LLM Applications
summary: An automated testing framework provides evidence-based release gates for LLM applications across five dimensions: task success, context preservation, latency, safety, and evidence coverage. Longitudinal evaluation on 20+ releases shows effective detection of regression-grade builds.
content:
arXiv:2603.15676v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: LLM applications are AI systems whose non-deterministic outputs and evolving model behavior make traditional testing insufficient for release governance. We present an automated self-testing framework that introduces quality gates with evidence-based release decisions (PROMOTE/HOLD/ROLLBACK) across five empirically grounded dimensions: task success rate, research context preservation, P95 latency, safety pass rate, and evidence coverage. We evaluate the framework through a longitudinal case study of an internally deployed multi-agent conversational AI system with specific marketing capabilities in active development, covering 38 evaluation runs across 20+ internal releases. The gate identified two ROLLBACK-grade builds in early runs and supported stable quality evolution over a four-week staging lifecycle while exercising persona-grounded, multi-turn, adversarial, and evidence-required scenarios. Statistical analysis (Mann-Kendall trends, Spearman correlations, bootstrap confidence intervals), gate ablation, and overhead scaling indicate that evidence coverage is the primary severe-regression discriminator and that runtime scales predictably with suite size. A human calibration study (n=60 stratified cases, two independent evaluators, LLM-as-judge cross-validation) reveals complementary multi-modal coverage: LLM-judge disagreements with the system gate (kappa=0.13) are attributable to structural failure modes such as latency violations and routing errors that are invisible in response text alone, while the judge independently surfaces content quality failures missed by structural checks, validating the multi-dimensional gate design. The framework, supplementary pseudocode, and calibration artifacts are provided to support AI-system quality assurance and independent replication.

zh.title: 自动自测试作为质量门：LLM应用程序的证据驱动发布管理
zh.summary: 自动化测试框架为LLM应用程序提供了基于证据的发布门，涵盖五个维度：任务成功、上下文保留、延迟、安全性和证据覆盖。对20多个发布版本的纵向评估显示有效检测回归级构建。

fr.title: Test Automatisé Automatique comme Porte de Qualité : Gestion des Versions Basée sur les Preuves pour les Applications LLM
fr.summary: Un cadre de test automatisé fournit des portes de libération basées sur les preuves pour les applications LLM sur cinq dimensions : succès des tâches, préservation du contexte, latence, sécurité et couverture des preuves. L'évaluation longitudinale sur 20+ versions montre une détection efficace des versions de type régression.

de.title: Automatisierte Selbsttests als Qualitätstor: Evidenzgesteuerte Releaseverwaltung für LLM-Anwendungen
de.summary: Ein automatisiertes Test-Framework bietet evidenzbasierte Release-Gates für LLM-Anwendungen über fünf Dimensionen: Task-Erfolg, Kontextbewahrung, Latenz, Sicherheit und Evidence Coverage. Die Längsevaluierung über 20+ Versionen zeigt eine effektive Erkennung von Regression-Builds.

es.title: Pruebas Automatizadas como Puerta de Calidad: Gestión de Versiones Basada en Evidencia para Aplicaciones LLM
es.summary: Un marco de pruebas automatizado proporciona puertas de lanzamiento basadas en evidencia para aplicaciones LLM en cinco dimensiones: éxito de tareas, preservación de contexto, latencia, seguridad y cobertura de evidencia. La evaluación longitudinal en 20+ versiones muestra una detección eficaz de compilaciones de regresión.

title: Spectral Edge Dynamics of Training Trajectories: Signal--Noise Geometry Across Scales
summary: Spectral Edge Dynamics reveals transformer training evolves within few coherent directions, with a sharp boundary separating signal from noise identified by singular value ratios. Universal three-phase patterns and lag phenomena provide early-warning signals for generalization across model scales.
content:
arXiv:2603.15678v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite hundreds of millions of parameters, transformer training trajectories evolve within only a few coherent directions. We introduce \emph{Spectral Edge Dynamics} (SED) to measure this structure: rolling-window SVD of parameter updates reveals a sharp boundary -- the \emph{spectral edge} -- between coherent optimization directions and stochastic noise, identified by the maximum consecutive singular value ratio $\sigma_k/\sigma_{k+1}$. Across a 51M-parameter TinyStories model (4~seeds) and GPT-2 124M under a distribution shift, the spectral edge exhibits a universal three-phase pattern (rise, plateau, collapse), signal rank adjusts with task complexity ($k^* = 2$ at 51M, $k^* = 3$ at 124M), and the directional coupling between spectral geometry and validation loss reverses with window size -- a \emph{lag flip} reflecting the timescale of trajectory integration. Johnson--Lindenstrauss projection to $d = 10W$ dimensions (e.g., $d = 100$ for $W = 10$) preserves the spectral gap within 5.7\%, making the framework applicable to models of arbitrary size. In companion work, the same spectral geometry provides early-warning signals of grokking -- predicting generalization 600--1{,}700 steps before it occurs across modular arithmetic, Dyck languages, and the SCAN benchmark.

zh.title: 训练轨迹的谱边动力学：跨尺度的信号-噪声几何
zh.summary: 谱边动力学揭示了变换器训练在少数相干方向内演变，通过奇异值比率识别信号与噪声的清晰边界。通用的三相模式和滞后现象为跨模型尺度的泛化提供早期预警信号。

fr.title: Dynamiques de bord spectral des trajectoires d'entraînement: géométrie signal-bruit à travers les échelles
fr.summary: La dynamique du bord spectral révèle que l'entraînement du transformateur évolue dans quelques directions cohérentes, avec une limite nette séparant le signal du bruit identifiée par les rapports de valeurs singulières. Les motifs universels en trois phases et les phénomènes de décalage fournissent des signaux d'alerte précoce pour la généralisation à travers les échelles de modèles.

de.title: Spektrale Randdynamik von Trainingstrajektorien: Signal-Rausch-Geometrie über Skalen
de.summary: Die spektrale Randdynamik zeigt, dass sich das Transformator-Training in wenigen kohärenten Richtungen entwickelt, mit einer scharfen Grenze, die Signal und Rauschen durch Singulärwertquotienten trennt. Universelle dreiphasige Muster und Verzögerungsphänomene bieten Frühwarnzeichen für die Verallgemeinerung über Modellskalen hinweg.

es.title: Dinámicas de borde espectral de trayectorias de entrenamiento: geometría de señal-ruido en diferentes escalas
es.summary: La dinámica de borde espectral revela que el entrenamiento del transformador evoluciona en pocas direcciones coherentes, con un límite nítido que separa la señal del ruido identificado por proporciones de valores singulares. Los patrones universales de tres fases y los fenómenos de retraso proporcionan señales de alerta temprana para la generalización en diferentes escalas de modelos.

title: IdentityGuard: Context-Aware Restriction and Provenance for Personalized Synthesis
summary: IdentityGuard enables context-aware safety restrictions for personalized text-to-image models, blocking harmful content only when combined with specific identities. A concept-specific watermark enables precise traceability while preserving model utility and preventing unintended collateral damage.
content:
arXiv:2603.15679v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The nature of personalized text-to-image models poses a unique safety challenge that generic context-blind methods are ill-equipped to handle. Such global filters create a dilemma: to prevent misuse, they are forced to damage the model's broader utility by erasing concepts entirely, causing unacceptable collateral damage.Our work presents a more precisely targeted approach, built on the principle that security should be as context-aware as the threat itself, intrinsically bound to the personalized concept. We present IDENTITYGUARD, which realizes this principle through a conditional restriction that blocks harmful content only when combined with the personalized identity, and a concept-specific watermark for precise traceability. Experiments show our approach prevents misuse while preserving the model's utility and enabling robust traceability. By moving beyond blunt, global filters, our work demonstrates a more effective and responsible path toward AI safety.

zh.title: IdentityGuard：个性化合成的上下文感知限制和来源
zh.summary: IdentityGuard为个性化的文本到图像模型启用了上下文感知的安全限制，仅在与特定身份结合时阻止有害内容。特定概念的水印实现了精确的可追溯性，同时保留模型效用并防止意外附带损害。

fr.title: IdentityGuard: restriction tenant compte du contexte et traçabilité pour la synthèse personnalisée
fr.summary: IdentityGuard permet des restrictions de sécurité tenant compte du contexte pour les modèles de synthèse d'images personnalisés, en bloquant le contenu nuisible uniquement lorsqu'il est combiné avec des identités spécifiques. Un filigrane spécifique au concept permet une traçabilité précise tout en préservant l'utilité du modèle et en prévenant les dommages collatéraux involontaires.

de.title: IdentityGuard: Kontextabhängige Einschränkung und Herkunft für personalisierte Synthese
de.summary: IdentityGuard ermöglicht kontextabhängige Sicherheitsbeschränkungen für personalisierte Text-zu-Bild-Modelle und blockiert schädliche Inhalte nur, wenn sie mit bestimmten Identitäten kombiniert werden. Ein konzeptspezifisches Wasserzeichen ermöglicht genaue Rückverfolgbarkeit und bewahrt gleichzeitig die Modelleffizienz und verhindert unbeabsichtigte Nebenschäden.

es.title: IdentityGuard: Restricción consciente del contexto y procedencia para síntesis personalizada
es.summary: IdentityGuard habilita restricciones de seguridad conscientes del contexto para modelos de síntesis de texto a imagen personalizados, bloqueando contenido dañino solo cuando se combina con identidades específicas. Una marca de agua específica del concepto permite una trazabilidad precisa mientras preserva la utilidad del modelo y previene daños colaterales involuntarios.

title: State-Dependent Safety Failures in Multi-Turn Language Model Interaction
summary: Multi-turn language model failures arise from conversational state evolution rather than isolated vulnerabilities, analyzed through the STAR state-oriented framework. Mechanistic analysis reveals monotonic drift from refusal mechanisms under structured multi-turn interaction.
content:
arXiv:2603.15684v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Safety alignment in large language models is typically evaluated under isolated queries, yet real-world use is inherently multi-turn. Although multi-turn jailbreaks are empirically effective, the structure of conversational safety failure remains insufficiently understood. In this work, we study safety failures from a state-space perspective and show that many multi-turn failures arise from structured contextual state evolution rather than isolated prompt vulnerabilities. We introduce STAR, a state-oriented diagnostic framework that treats dialogue history as a state transition operator and enables controlled analysis of safety behavior along interaction trajectories. Rather than optimizing attack strength, STAR provides a principled probe of how aligned models traverse the safety boundary under autoregressive conditioning. Across multiple frontier language models, we find that systems that appear robust under static evaluation can undergo rapid and reproducible safety collapse under structured multi-turn interaction. Mechanistic analysis reveals monotonic drift away from refusal-related representations and abrupt phase transitions induced by role-conditioned context. Together, these findings motivate viewing language model safety as a dynamic, state-dependent process defined over conversational trajectories.

zh.title: 多轮语言模型交互中的状态相关安全失败
zh.summary: 多轮语言模型失败源于对话状态演变而非孤立的漏洞，通过STAR状态导向框架进行分析。机制分析揭示了结构化多轮交互下拒绝机制的单调漂移。

fr.title: Défaillances de sécurité dépendantes de l'état dans l'interaction multi-tours du modèle de langage
fr.summary: Les défaillances des modèles de langage multi-tours résultent de l'évolution de l'état conversationnel plutôt que de vulnérabilités isolées, analysées par le cadre orienté état STAR. L'analyse mécaniste révèle une dérive monotone des mécanismes de refus sous l'interaction multi-tours structurée.

de.title: Zustandsabhängige Sicherheitsausfälle in Multi-Turn-Sprachmodellinteraktion
de.summary: Multi-Turn-Sprachmodellfehler entstehen durch die Entwicklung des Konversationszustands und nicht durch isolierte Schwachstellen, analysiert durch das STAR-Zustandsorientierungsframework. Die mechanistische Analyse offenbart eine monotone Drift von Ablehnungsmechanismen bei strukturierter Multi-Turn-Interaktion.

es.title: Fallos de seguridad dependientes del estado en la interacción de modelos de lenguaje de múltiples turnos
es.summary: Los fallos de modelos de lenguaje de múltiples turnos surgen de la evolución del estado conversacional en lugar de vulnerabilidades aisladas, analizados a través del marco orientado al estado STAR. El análisis mecanístico revela una deriva monótona de mecanismos de rechazo bajo la interacción estructurada de múltiples turnos.
